Syllabi.title: Structured Syllabi
Syllabi.subtitle: Competitive programming roadmap — from basics to advanced techniques
Syllabi.courseCompleteDesc: You have completed the entire path of mastery!
Syllabi.tableError: Roadmap topics table not found. Please check database schema.
Syllabi.articleInProgressDesc: Article content is being prepared. Check back soon or contribute via GitHub.
Syllabi.practiceDesc: Problems are curated by topic and solved directly on the OlympLab platform in the built-in IDE.
Syllabi.noProblemsDesc: Challenges for this topic haven't been added to the database yet. Check back soon for new training material.
Syllabi.layerIntroDesc: Core concepts and classic problems to understand the topic
Syllabi.layerMixedDesc: Hard problems and combinations with other topics

Landing.badge: An open lab for competitive programmers
Landing.heroSubtitle: A focused workspace for competitive programming: a Monaco-powered IDE, real Codeforces & CSES judging, a Gemini AI mentor that guides instead of spoiling, a structured roadmap, and 20+ interactive algorithm visualizers.

ide.mentorText: Your O(n) idea is right. Before you code the range queries, what could you precompute so each query becomes O(1)?

Landing.featuresSub: Every part of the platform exists to shorten the loop between reading a problem and understanding why your solution works.

features.ideDesc: Monaco editor with syntax highlighting and five languages — C++, Python, Java, Rust and Go — plus your own code templates. Run against the samples in a single keystroke.
features.mentorTitle: An AI mentor that won't spoil
features.mentorDesc: A Gemini-powered mentor built on Pólya's method. It diagnoses your thinking and gives graduated hints — the next step, never the whole solution.
features.judgeDesc: Submit straight to Codeforces and CSES through the platform, or get an instant verdict checked against the sample tests. Status and verdicts stream back live.
features.roadmapDesc: Follow a staged syllabus from fundamentals to advanced DP and graphs, with readable articles and curated problems linked to every topic.
features.visualizersDesc: Watch Dijkstra, segment trees, DSU, sorting and more run step by step, so the algorithm becomes intuition instead of memorized code.
features.analyticsDesc: A GitHub-style activity heatmap, verdict analytics, streaks and full submission history keep your weak spots and momentum in plain view.

Landing.visualizersHeading: See the algorithm, not just the code
Landing.visualizersSub: Over twenty hand-built visualizers animate the data structures and techniques that show up again and again in contests.
Landing.howHeading: From problem to intuition in four steps

how.step1Desc: Start from the roadmap or jump into a problem matched to your current skill level.
how.step2Desc: Write, run against samples, and submit — to Codeforces, CSES, or the instant judge.
how.step3Desc: Ask the AI mentor for the next hint, or replay the relevant visualizer until it clicks.
how.step4Desc: Watch your heatmap fill in and your verdict analytics sharpen over time.

Landing.mentorSub: The mentor follows a hint ladder: a diagnostic question first, a stronger nudge only if you're still stuck, and a full walkthrough only when you ask for it.
Landing.mentorPoint1: Pinpoints where your reasoning breaks down
Landing.mentorPoint2: Reviews your code — idea, correctness, complexity, edge cases
Landing.mentorPoint3: Reveals the full solution only when you explicitly ask

extras.spoilerDesc: Hide tags and difficulty to recreate real contest conditions.
extras.adaptiveDesc: A skill-axis matching engine suggests problems at the edge of your ability.
extras.toolsDesc: Sketch graphs and reason visually without leaving the browser.
extras.bilingualDesc: Use the entire platform in English or Russian.

Landing.ctaSub: Free to start. No credit card required.
